findings.message: test_resolved_log_level_prod_is_info is a verbatim duplicate of an existing parametrized case. test_resolved_log_level_is_info_outside_dev is already parametrized over ["staging", "prod", "ci"] and asserts the identical expression `Settings(environment="prod").resolved_log_level == "INFO"`. The new test cannot fail in any scenario where the existing parametrized test passes; it adds zero coverage and will never catch a regression that the suite does not already catch.
findings.suggestion: Delete test_resolved_log_level_prod_is_info entirely. If the intent is to document the security rationale (no DEBUG in prod leaks data), add the comment to test_resolved_log_level_is_info_outside_dev instead.

findings.message: test_slo_request_latency_p99_env_override exercises the same Pydantic-settings env-override mechanism already proven by test_env_vars_override (which covers APP_SLO_ERROR_RATE_PCT). The new test does cover a distinct field name, so it is not a pure duplicate, but the marginal confidence gained is low: a bug that silently ignores APP_SLO_REQUEST_LATENCY_P99_MS would be the only new failure mode caught, and that scenario is already guarded by test_defaults asserting the default value. Consider whether this test pulls its weight.
findings.suggestion: Either fold APP_SLO_REQUEST_LATENCY_P99_MS into the existing test_env_vars_override (add a third setenv/assert pair) to consolidate the env-override story in one place, or keep it but remove the comment claiming it is 'meaningful' for a reason that applies equally to every other env-override test in the file.
